title: Automatic Storage, Recursion, VLAs, and Escaping Addresses · Guided Pointer Studio
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working bounded stack-use redesign while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded stack-use redesign while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep pointers to automatic objects within the execution and subobject lifetimes that make them valid.
Bound recursion by adversarial input depth, frame cost, and target stack limits or replace it with explicit storage.
Use variable-length arrays only under explicit positive bounds and stack budgets, with portable alternatives.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

hints: For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Escape Analysis Review, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Escape Analysis Review precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Escape Analysis Review, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 4354. Produce bounded C evidence to replace unbounded recursive work with bounded explicit storage; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace every stored or returned pointer back to its referent lifetime, including compound literals and callback contexts.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Escape Analysis Review, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: Only return statements can cause a local address to escape.

stimulus.title: Escape Analysis Review failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Only return statements can cause a local address to escape.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace every stored or returned pointer back to its referent lifetime, including compound literals and callback contexts.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Escape Analysis Review condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Escape Analysis Review case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Escape Analysis Review
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Escape Analysis Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 5973. Produce bounded C evidence to replace unbounded recursive work with bounded explicit storage; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace every stored or returned pointer back to its referent lifetime, including compound literals and callback contexts.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Escape Analysis Review.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Escape Analysis Review, keep Escape Analysis Review connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Trace every stored or returned pointer back to its referent lifetime, including compound literals and callback contexts.
